name: Egypt

colors.symbolism: the period before the 1952 revolution and the sacrifices of those who fought British rule
the bloodless nature of the 1952 revolution
the end of the era of foreign occupation and oppression
from the gilded eagle of Saladin

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — red over white over black — with a gold Eagle of Saladin centred on the white stripe. The eagle carries a shield charged with the national colours on its breast and stands above a scroll bearing the country's name in Arabic.
history: The red-white-black tricolour is the Arab Liberation Flag introduced by the Free Officers after the 23 July 1952 revolution that overthrew King Farouk; it was first used by the United Arab Republic (Egypt and Syria) from 1958. The current design — a plain tricolour bearing the Eagle of Saladin — was adopted on 4 October 1984, replacing the earlier Federation of Arab Republics hawk (1972–1984). The eagle takes its name from a heraldic motif found on the western wall of Saladin's Citadel in Cairo.

features.symbolism: the colours of the Arab Liberation Flag — red for the bloody struggle against oppression, white for the bright future and the bloodless 1952 revolution, black for the dark past of foreign domination

strength, power, and the link to Saladin (Salah ad-Din), the 12th-century sultan who united the Arabs against the Crusaders; on its breast a shield in the national colours, and below a scroll bearing the country's name in Kufic Arabic — جمهورية مصر العربية (Arab Republic of Egypt)
